page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highmem-64 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the n4d family. It features 64 dedicated virtual CPUs and 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. This high memory density classifies the instance as a memory-optimized configuration.

The instance does not include local NVMe SSD storage, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. It also lacks integrated GPU hardware accelerators and does not bundle complimentary public IPv4 addresses. These constraints mean workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space or hardware-accelerated parallel processing must look to alternative configurations or attach external resources.

From a qualitative cost efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation combined with a high memory-to-core ratio provides a dense resource footprint for memory-bound applications. The instance is designed for workloads such as large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems, enterprise resource planning applications, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines that do not require local disk storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highmem-64 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the n4d family, configured with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads. The instance does not include local storage, integrated GPUs, or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, meaning all storage must be network-attached. Qualitatively, the dedicated CPU allocation and high memory density offer a specialized resource profile for applications requiring substantial memory capacity without the overhead of local physical disks. It is designed for workloads such as in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and memory-heavy data processing tasks.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server configuration featuring sixty-four dedicated virtual CPUs and five hundred twelve gigabytes of system memory.

Zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included
